codeReview.summary: The user-management-app implementation is mostly complete functionally, but has critical SECURITY issues that block user testing: (1) Password hashing uses SHA-256 instead of bcrypt as specified in AGENTS.md - this is a critical vulnerability since SHA-256 is too fast for password hashing and susceptible to brute-force attacks. (2) Login attempt tracking is stored in-memory only (JavaScript Map) which means lockout status is lost on app restart - defeating the 30-minute lockout security feature. (3) Session idle timeout is only enforced client-side, not server-side, so sessions can be extended indefinitely via refreshSession.

issues.description: Password hashing uses SHA-256 (crypto.createHash('sha256')) instead of bcrypt as explicitly required by AGENTS.md 'Security' section. SHA-256 is inappropriate for password hashing because it's too fast (vulnerable to brute-force) and lacks salting. This is a critical security vulnerability.

Login attempt tracking (loginAttempts Map) is stored in-memory only. When the app restarts, all lockout status is lost - a user who was locked out for 29 minutes can immediately try again after an app restart. The 30-minute lockout feature is defeated by simply restarting the application.

refreshSession() allows indefinite session extension with no max session lifetime. The 15-minute idle timeout specified in the feature is not enforced server-side - a client can keep refreshing forever. The validateSession query checks expires_at but refreshSession can always extend it.

Database path uses app.getPath('userData') which resolves to %APPDATA%/SoftwareSawit/data/users.db instead of project-relative /data/users.db as specified in feature description and AGENTS.md. This is noted in handoff as 'non_blocking' but represents a documented requirement violation.
logActivity() does not call saveDatabase() after inserting. Activity logs are only saved when other operations trigger a save. During a crash or forced shutdown, recent activity logs may be lost.

validatePasswordStrength only checks: 8+ chars, uppercase, number. Feature description mentions 'password management with strength validation' but doesn't specify special character requirements. Consider adding special character validation for stronger passwords.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md 'Security' section explicitly states 'Hash passwords with bcrypt' but the implementation uses SHA-256. This is a documented convention that was not followed.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/main/userDatabase.ts:130 - hashPassword() uses crypto.createHash('sha256'). AGENTS.md Security section line 4: 'Hash passwords with bcrypt'

sharedStateObservations.observation: backend-worker skill specifies 'TDD Phase (Write Tests First)' but no tests were added. Handoff shows 'tests: { added: [], coverage: 'Manual testing required...' }'. This is a deviation from documented skill procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff discoveredIssues notes 'Manual testing required via agent-browser'. Skill file backend-worker/SKILL.md Section 2: 'TDD Phase (Write Tests First)'

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md specifies database path as project-relative /data/users.db but implementation uses %APPDATA%/SoftwareSawit/data/users.db via app.getPath('userData'). This path also requires mkdir with recursive option, but AGENTS.md doesn't mention this platform-specific detail.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/main/userDatabase.ts:35 - DB_PATH = path.join(app.getPath('userData'), 'data', 'users.db'). AGENTS.md Database Schema shows /data/users.yaml (note: says .yaml but likely means .db)

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker skill 'backend-worker' was updated during this commit (shown in git diff), adding detailed database schema. The worker followed the updated skill but the previous version may have been used initially.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Git diff shows .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md was modified in same commit 41b87fc

summary: FAIL - Critical security issues prevent user testing: (1) SHA-256 password hashing must be replaced with bcrypt per AGENTS.md spec, (2) Login attempt lockout tracking must persist across app restarts, (3) Server-side idle timeout enforcement needed. Functional implementation is mostly complete with user CRUD, role assignment, session management, activity logging, and Excel export working, but security issues are blocking.
